title: Fix persistent transcript accumulation: reset currentTranscript in stopListening()
description:
Voice tab accumulates all utterances into progressively longer messages WITHIN a single listening session (mic not tapped multiple times).

## Root Cause
SFSpeechRecognizer returns CUMULATIVE bestTranscriptionString for the lifetime of a recognition request. restartRecognition() was added but may not be working correctly — possible issues:
1. Old task fires a final callback AFTER restart, repopulating currentTranscript with full accumulated text
2. The 'utterance offset' approach is needed: track where the last emitted utterance ended, only send the NEW portion
3. stopListening() still doesn't clear currentTranscript

## Recommended Fix: Utterance Offset Tracking
Instead of restarting the task (which has timing hazards), track the last emitted transcript length and only send words after that offset:

var lastEmittedLength = 0

In emitUtteranceIfValid():
  let full = currentTranscript.trimmingCharacters(...)
  let newPortion = String(full.dropFirst(lastEmittedLength)).trimmingCharacters(...)
  guard newPortion.count >= minimumTranscriptLength else { return }
  onUtteranceComplete?(newPortion)
  lastEmittedLength = full.count

In stopListening() / startListening():
  lastEmittedLength = 0
  currentTranscript = ''

This is safe even if the old task fires late callbacks.

Silence timer dies after empty emission — STT waits forever
description:
After the user finishes speaking and the utterance is emitted, the silence timer can permanently self-destruct, leaving the VoiceEngine listening with no timer to detect future silence.

Sequence:
1. User speaks, partials arrive, hasReceivedPartial = true, watchdog cancelled permanently
2. Silence timer fires → emitUtteranceIfValid() → text emitted → restartRecognition() called
3. restartRecognition() arms a new silence timer (line 412)
4. No new partials arrive from the restarted recognition task
5. The silence timer fires again → emitUtteranceIfValid()
6. currentTranscript = '', pendingTranscript = '' (both cleared by step 2)
7. buildFullTranscript() returns '' (< 3 chars)
8. Guard at line 334 rejects it, method returns at line 337
9. BUT: the timer was already invalidated at line 330-331 and NO new timer is scheduled
10. Watchdog was cancelled at step 1 (hasReceivedPartial = true, never reset)
11. Engine is listening with NO timer. If user speaks again, partials re-arm it. If not, it waits forever.

The gap: emitUtteranceIfValid() invalidates the silence timer (line 330-331) then returns without rescheduling if the text is too short (line 334-337). And the watchdog only runs during cold-start (hasReceivedPartial is never reset to false).

Fix options:
- In emitUtteranceIfValid(), always reschedule the silence timer (or call restartRecognition) even when text is too short
- Reset hasReceivedPartial = false in restartRecognition() so the watchdog can re-arm
- Add a secondary 'idle watchdog' that fires if no partials arrive for N seconds regardless of hasReceivedPartial
- Re-arm the silence timer at the end of emitUtteranceIfValid() unconditionally

Key file: VoiceEngine.swift (emitUtteranceIfValid lines 329-343, resetSilenceTimer, watchdog, hasReceivedPartial)

title: Fix error routing: non-auth errors misclassified as challengeFailed
description:
GatewayConnection treats ALL failed response messages as .challengeFailed, which triggers needsRepairingPublisher and wipes pairing state. Only NOT_PAIRED and PAIRING_REQUIRED errors should trigger re-pairing.

## Current State (after zaap-bgg fix)
Zane patched the coordinator side in commit 63cd096: VoiceChatCoordinator.gatewayDidFailWithError now string-matches on msg.hasPrefix("pairing_required") || msg.hasPrefix("NOT_PAIRED") before firing needsRepairingPublisher. This stops the immediate symptom (pairing view appearing every launch) but the fix relies on string matching at the coordinator level rather than proper error typing at the source.

## Remaining Problem
GatewayConnection.handleRawMessage (lines 284-306) still reports ALL failed 'res' messages as .challengeFailed — the else branch at line 304-305 fires .challengeFailed(msg) for ANY error that isn't NOT_PAIRED or PAIRING_REQUIRED. This means the type system doesn't distinguish between auth failures and ordinary request failures. Any future delegate consumer would need to repeat the same string-matching logic.

## Required Changes
1. Add a new error variant (e.g. .requestFailed(String)) to GatewayConnectionError for non-auth errors
2. In handleRawMessage, only use .challengeFailed for NOT_PAIRED/PAIRING_REQUIRED; use .requestFailed for everything else
3. Update VoiceChatCoordinator.gatewayDidFailWithError to match on the error variant instead of string prefix (simplifies Zane's fix)
4. Log .requestFailed errors for debugging but don't wipe pairing state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New GatewayConnectionError variant exists for non-auth failures
- [ ] INVALID_REQUEST errors are reported as .requestFailed, not .challengeFailed
- [ ] NOT_PAIRED and PAIRING_REQUIRED errors still reported as .challengeFailed
- [ ] Coordinator matches on error variant, not string prefix
- [ ] All existing tests pass + new tests covering error classification
- [ ] Test: .challengeFailed only fires for NOT_PAIRED/PAIRING_REQUIRED
- [ ] Test: .requestFailed fires for INVALID_REQUEST and other non-auth errors

title: Fix voice transcript accumulation — restart recognition task after each utterance
description:
Every voice prompt sent to the gateway contains the full accumulated transcript since listening started, not just the current utterance.

## Root Cause
In VoiceEngine.emitUtteranceIfValid():
1. Emits the transcript via onUtteranceComplete
2. Clears currentTranscript = ""
3. BUT does NOT restart the recognition task

SFSpeechRecognizer recognition tasks accumulate text for their entire lifetime. The next result.bestTranscriptionString callback restores the full accumulated text, overwriting the cleared currentTranscript.

## Fix
After emitting an utterance, restart the recognition task:
- Cancel and nil out the current recognitionTask and recognitionRequest
- Start a new recognition request immediately (so listening continues without interruption)
- The new task starts with an empty transcript

In emitUtteranceIfValid(), after clearing currentTranscript, call a restartRecognition() method that tears down the current task and starts a fresh one.

## Files
- Zaap/Zaap/VoiceEngine.swift: emitUtteranceIfValid(), add restartRecognition()

title: TTS doesn't stop when mic/conversation is toggled off
description:
When the user toggles conversation mode off or stops the session while TTS is speaking, the TTS continues playing. Both toggleConversationMode() and stopSession() call speaker.interrupt(), but it appears to not always stop the audio.

Root cause analysis: ResponseSpeaker.interrupt() (line 132-138) has a guard:
  guard state == .speaking else { return }
If the internal state has already transitioned to .idle (e.g. the AVSpeechSynthesizer delegate fired didFinish right before the toggle), the guard skips the stopSpeaking() call. But AVSpeechSynthesizer may still have queued utterances — didFinish fires per-utterance, not for the entire queue.

The issue is in how state is managed relative to AVSpeechSynthesizer's internal queue:
- bufferToken() calls speakImmediate() for each extracted sentence
- Each speakImmediate() calls synthesizer.speak(utterance) and sets state = .speaking
- AVSpeechSynthesizer queues these internally
- didFinish fires after EACH utterance completes
- handleDidFinish checks if synthesizer.isSpeaking — if the next queued utterance hasn't started yet, isSpeaking may briefly be false
- state is set to .idle
- The next queued utterance starts speaking — but state is already .idle
- Now interrupt() is called — guard sees state == .idle, skips stopSpeaking()

Fix: Don't guard interrupt() on state. Always call synthesizer.stopSpeaking(at: .immediate) regardless of internal state. Or check synthesizer.isSpeaking directly instead of the managed state property.

Key files:
- ResponseSpeaker.swift (interrupt, speakImmediate, handleDidFinish, state management)
- VoiceChatCoordinator.swift (toggleConversationMode line 217, stopSession line 233)

description: When TTS finishes and conversation mode is on, the mic restarts immediately with no delay. This can catch the tail end of TTS audio in the buffer, causing echo — especially on simulator where there is no hardware AEC. Add a small delay (e.g. 500ms) after speaker goes idle before calling voiceEngine.startListening(). Guard against conversation mode being toggled off during the delay.

title: NodePairingManager — Ed25519 keypair, Keychain storage, pairing WS flow
description: Implement NodePairingManager: generate Ed25519 keypair on first launch and store in iOS Keychain, initiate node.pair.request over WebSocket (nodeId=sha256(publicKey), displayName='Zaap (iPhone)', platform='iOS', caps=['voice']), handle pairing approval/rejection from gateway, store issued token in Keychain. Follows the pairing flow in .braids/iterations/005/z75-voice-chat-architecture.md.

title: STT resets transcript mid-speech, sending partial sentences
description:
While the user is speaking, the STT transcript sometimes resets — the displayed partial text disappears and starts re-accumulating from scratch. This leads to partial/truncated sentences being sent to the gateway.

Root cause: Apple's SFSpeechRecognizer fires isFinal mid-speech (typically after brief pauses or when its internal buffer fills). The handleIsFinal() path saves the transcript as pendingTranscript and calls restartRecognition(), which clears currentTranscript. A debounce timer (silenceThreshold = 1.5s) is started. If no new speech arrives before the debounce fires, the pending transcript is emitted as a complete utterance — even though the user was mid-sentence.

The carry-forward mechanism (VoiceEngine.swift:386-398) attempts to merge pendingTranscript with new speech, but only works if new partials arrive BEFORE the 1.5s debounce timer fires. Natural pauses in speech (commas, thinking, breathing) can exceed 1.5s, causing premature emission.

Visible behavior:
1. User says 'I was thinking about going to the store and...'
2. isFinal fires after 'store'
3. Debounce timer starts (1.5s)
4. User pauses briefly (>1.5s)
5. 'I was thinking about going to the store' is emitted as a complete message
6. User continues '...and picking up some groceries' — this becomes a separate message

Possible fixes:
- Increase silenceThreshold / debounce time to 2.5-3s to tolerate natural pauses
- Make the silence threshold configurable in Settings
- Don't treat isFinal as a signal to emit — instead, only use the silence timer
- Show a visual indicator of the silence countdown so the user can see when their message will be sent
- Use a smarter heuristic: if the transcript ends mid-clause (no terminal punctuation), extend the timer

Key files:
- VoiceEngine.swift (handleIsFinal, emitPendingTranscript, silenceThreshold, restartRecognition)

title: Fix mid-sentence cutoff sending partial messages
description:
During a voice session, the user gets cut off mid-sentence and partial messages are sent to the agent. This happens because:

1. Apple's SFSpeechRecognizer fires isFinal on a recognition result mid-sentence, which triggers emitUtteranceIfValid() (VoiceEngine.swift:194-196), immediately sending whatever has been said so far.
2. Recognition then restarts, and if the restart is slow, the watchdog (3s) or silence timer (1.5s) can fire again on the next fragment.
3. The user sees 'No speech detected' warnings during these restart gaps, and their sentence gets split across multiple messages.

The net effect: speaking at a normal pace produces fragmented messages. Only speaking very fast gets a full sentence through.

Possible fixes:
- Debounce or buffer isFinal results — don't emit immediately on isFinal, instead wait briefly for more speech
- Increase silenceThreshold from 1.5s to something more forgiving (2-3s), or make it user-configurable
- When recognition restarts mid-speech, carry forward the existing transcript rather than resetting lastEmittedLength
- Distinguish between Apple's natural isFinal (end of utterance) and timeout-induced isFinal (recognition ran too long)

Key files:
- VoiceEngine.swift (silenceThreshold, emitUtteranceIfValid, restartRecognition, isFinal handling)
- VoiceChatCoordinator.swift (handleUtteranceComplete, sendVoiceTranscript)
close_reason: Superseded by zaap-aql (transcript reset) which has a more thorough analysis of the mid-sentence cutoff root cause.

title: Pairing prompt appears every launch despite being already paired
description: On real device, the pairing view shows every time even though the device is already paired. Root causes to investigate: (1) VoiceChatView.onAppear creates a NEW NodePairingManager() each time, which creates a new RealKeychain instance — token lookup may fail if keychain query params differ from save. (2) needsRepairingPublisher fires on challengeFailed and calls clearPairing(), which deletes the token — if the challenge fails for any reason (timing, nonce, etc.) it wipes pairing state. (3) Check RealKeychain save/load symmetry: are the kSecAttrSynchronizable flags consistent between save and load?

Script lives in scripts/pre-push (tracked), symlinked into .git/hooks/pre-push. AGENTS.md should document the setup step.

Should include: 1) What Zaap does (personal telemetry relay — location, sleep, heart rate, activity, workouts). 2) How to build and install via TestFlight. 3) CRITICAL: Step-by-step guide for another OpenClaw instance to set up all the webhook mappings this app calls (/hooks/location, /hooks/sleep, /hooks/heartrate, /hooks/activity, /hooks/workout). Include example openclaw.json hooks config with all 5 mappings and suggested messageTemplates. 4) App settings (hostname, bearer token). 5) Privacy/permissions overview.

title: First mic press produces no speech — must toggle off/on
description:
The first mic tap starts the audio engine and SFSpeechRecognizer, but the recognizer consistently produces zero partials on its initial recognition task. The watchdog (3s) fires, restarts recognition, and the cycle repeats 3-5 times before speech finally gets through. The user has to toggle the mic off then on again to get it working.

The zaap-yxj fix suppressed the 'No speech detected' error display, but didn't solve the underlying problem: SFSpeechRecognizer's on-device model is not ready when the first recognition task is created.

Log evidence (14:21:02-14:21:08):
  startListening → now listening → watchdog: no partials after 3.0s → restart → suppressing cold-start error → repeat 3x → user gives up and toggles off

This also happens after every TTS playback when the mic restarts — see log at 14:21:24-14:21:30 and 14:21:42-14:21:52.

Possible fixes:
- Pre-warm SFSpeechRecognizer on app launch or on first onAppear (create and immediately cancel a dummy recognition task to load the model)
- Add a warm-up recognition task in startListening() that runs for a brief period before the real task
- Use a longer initial watchdog (e.g. 5s on first attempt) to give the model time to load
- Keep a recognition task alive in the background (paused) so the model stays hot

Key files:
- VoiceEngine.swift (startListening, watchdog, restartRecognition)
- VoiceEngineAdapters.swift (RealSpeechRecognizer, RealAudioEngineProvider)

title: Add HRV (Heart Rate Variability) tracking: ObservedHealthDataType, delivery service, and /hooks/hrv gateway route
Mute mic while speaker is talking to prevent feedback loop
description:
The TTS speaker output is being picked up by the microphone, causing the assistant to respond to itself in a loop.

## Root Cause
VoiceEngine continues listening while ResponseSpeaker is playing TTS. The speaker audio feeds back into SFSpeechRecognizer.

## Fix
Pause/stop listening when the speaker starts talking, resume when it finishes.

In VoiceChatCoordinator:
- When speaker state changes to .speaking → call voiceEngine.stopListening()
- When speaker finishes (speakImmediate/flush completes) → call voiceEngine.startListening() if session is still active

ResponseSpeaker should notify when it finishes speaking. The delegate/callback pattern already exists (onUtteranceComplete in VoiceEngine). ResponseSpeaker needs a similar completion callback, OR VoiceChatCoordinator should check speaker.state before restarting the mic.

## Current flow (broken)
listening → transcript sent → speaking starts → mic still on → picks up TTS → loop

## Correct flow
listening → transcript sent → stop mic → speaking starts → speaking ends → resume mic

title: Location manager — CLLocationManager with significant location change monitoring
Change heart rate observer frequency from immediate to hourly
TTS stutters/hiccups at start of response
description:
The TTS voice stutters, typically 1-2 seconds into speaking a response. This is caused by the ResponseSpeaker architecture: each sentence is spoken as a separate AVSpeechUtterance queued to AVSpeechSynthesizer.

When a chat final event arrives, the entire response text is passed to bufferToken(), which splits it into sentences on '.', '!', '?' boundaries and calls speakImmediate() for each. Then flush() speaks any trailing fragment. This rapidly enqueues multiple AVSpeechUtterance objects.

AVSpeechSynthesizer processes these sequentially, but transitions between utterances produce audible gaps or stutters. The first utterance may hiccup when subsequent utterances are queued while it's still being synthesized.

Additional issues:
- Naive sentence splitting on '.' causes false splits (e.g. 'Mr. Smith' → 'Mr.' + ' Smith')
- No pre/post utterance delay set (defaults to 0)
- The existing TTSAudioPlayer (uses AVSpeechSynthesizer.write() + AVAudioPlayerNode for buffer-based playback) would provide smoother output but is not wired in

Possible fixes:
- Combine all sentences into a single AVSpeechUtterance instead of splitting
- Add small preUtteranceDelay between queued utterances
- Switch to TTSAudioPlayer which delivers PCM buffers through AVAudioPlayerNode for seamless playback
- Improve sentence extraction to handle abbreviations (Mr., Dr., etc.)
- For streaming responses, buffer more text before starting TTS

Key files:
- ResponseSpeaker.swift (bufferToken, speakImmediate, extractSentences, flush)
- TTSAudioPlayer.swift (alternative buffer-based approach)
- VoiceChatCoordinator.swift (handleChatEvent, lines 445-451)

title: Remove redundant Summary section from Settings
Default to #general session instead of Main
description: Main session has too much automated activity (heartbeats, crons, hooks). After sessions load, auto-select the discord #general session if available, falling back to Main only if #general isn't found.

title: Allow both debug panels (STT + TTS) active simultaneously
description: The gear menu toggles for STT and TTS debug panels should be independent — both can be on at the same time. When both are active, the TTS panel plays The Raven while the STT panel shows what the mic picks up, providing a direct visual echo measurement. Panels stack vertically at the top of the voice tab.

title: Improve UX when switching channels while mic is hot
description:
When the user switches Discord channels (via session picker) while the mic is active, the session key updates correctly and old-session events are silently dropped. However the UX is poor:

1. No visual feedback that the session switched — old responses just disappear silently
2. Any in-flight partial transcript is discarded rather than being sent to the old session before switching
3. The log fills with hundreds of 'session key mismatch' drop messages (observed: 999 lines of drops in a single log capture)

Current behavior (VoiceChatCoordinator.swift:146-169 updateSessionKey):
- Stops mic, interrupts TTS, cancels mic restart
- Clears partial transcript and resets VM to idle
- Schedules mic restart if conversation mode was on
- Old session events are filtered out at lines 357-359 and 394

Possible improvements:
- Flush pending transcript to the OLD session before switching keys
- Show a brief toast/indicator that the session changed
- Consider whether the mic should auto-restart on the new session or require explicit user action
- Reduce log noise by batching or rate-limiting the session-mismatch log messages

Key files:
- VoiceChatCoordinator.swift (updateSessionKey, event filtering)
- VoiceChatView.swift (onChange of selectedSessionKey, lines 115-123)
- GatewayConnection.swift (event delivery)

title: Voice integration — wire VoiceEngine → GatewayConnection → ResponseSpeaker
description: Wire up the full voice pipeline: VoiceEngine transcript → GatewayConnection voice.transcript event → receive chat.event stream → ResponseSpeaker speaks response. Handle interrupts (user speaks while TTS playing). Add pairing UI to Settings (enter gateway address, pair button, paired status). Check WorkoutReader.swift for the WorkoutError.noData case and update the errorDescription. Also check if the UI surfaces this message anywhere and update accordingly.

description: Hardware AEC via .voiceChat mode is not sufficient on iPhone — TTS output is still picked up by STT. Headphones work but aren't acceptable UX. Options: (1) Mute mic during TTS but allow interrupt via button tap instead of voice. (2) Software echo filtering: track TTS output text, ignore STT transcripts that match. (3) Explore AVAudioEngine voice processing tap with manual AEC. Need to decide approach and implement.

title: Polish pairing view UX for dual-role flow
description: Pairing view now handles node+operator sequentially but the status messages are bare. Improve copy and flow — e.g. progress indicator showing which step (1/2), friendlier language, maybe a brief explanation of what each role does.

title: Add discreet 'Copy transcript' button to voice tab for debugging
description:
Add a small, unobtrusive button in the voice tab that copies the last 10 conversation messages to the clipboard as plain text. Used for sharing transcript state without screenshots.

## Requirements
- Small, subtle UI — not prominent. E.g. a tiny clipboard icon (doc.on.doc) in the corner, or a long-press gesture on the transcript area
- Copies last 10 entries from the conversation log (user + assistant messages)
- Format: each line as '[User]: text' or '[Zane]: text'
- Shows brief 'Copied!' feedback (UIPasteboard + overlay toast or .alert)
- Dev-only is fine but not required

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Button/gesture exists in VoiceChatView
- [ ] Copies last min(10, messages.count) entries to UIPasteboard.general.string
- [ ] Brief visual confirmation shown after copy
- [ ] Grep VoiceChatView.swift for UIPasteboard — must be present

title: Fix session picker race condition and continuation leak
description: Session picker is empty due to multiple issues: (1) Race condition: loadSessions() called in gatewayDidConnect() via async Task, but gateway may disconnect before it runs. listSessions() checks state==.connected and throws, error is silently caught. (2) pendingSessionListContinuation is never cleaned up on disconnect — request hangs forever. (3) sessionPicker is a weak var that could be nil when gatewayDidConnect fires. Fix: clean up continuation on disconnect (resume with error), add state check before loadSessions, consider strong reference for sessionPicker.

title: Fix mic button permanently disabled when no Discord sessions load from gateway
description:
The mic button is permanently disabled because no sessions pass the Discord filter — channelType field is missing from gateway response.

## Root Cause
gateway sessions.list response does NOT include a 'channelType' field. The filter:
  sessions = result.filter { $0.channelType == "discord" }
...drops ALL sessions since channelType is always nil.

Session key format reveals channel: 'agent:main:discord:CHANNELID'

## Fix
1. Derive channelType from session key in handleSessionListResponse:
   - key contains ':discord:' → channelType = 'discord'
   - key == 'agent:main:main' → channelType = 'main'
   - else → channelType = other

2. Remove the Discord-only filter — show all sessions.

3. Auto-select default session — do NOT require manual selection:
   - First preference: key == 'agent:main:main' (always exists in OpenClaw)
   - Fallback: most recently active session
   - Mic button always enabled regardless of selection state

4. Keep the picker visible so the user CAN switch sessions, but the main session is pre-selected.

## Files
- Zaap/Zaap/GatewayConnection.swift (handleSessionListResponse — derive channelType from key)
- Zaap/Zaap/SessionPickerViewModel.swift (auto-select main session, remove Discord filter, remove isSessionSelected gate)

title: Voice pairing: Use gateway bearer token from settings instead of hardcoded fallback
Dashboard: fix X-axis to always show 7 days
description: The chart X-axis auto-scales based on data, showing hours when only today has data. It should always show exactly 7 days (today minus 6 days through end of today). Use .chartXScale(domain: sevenDaysAgo...endOfToday) to fix the domain. Also use .chartXAxis with AxisMarks(values: .stride(by: .day)) to force day-level labels. This makes the chart always read as a 7-day view regardless of how much data exists.

Each delivery service appends its own path segment: LocationDeliveryService posts to /location, SleepDeliveryService to /sleep, HeartRateDeliveryService to /heartrate, ActivityDeliveryService to /activity, WorkoutDeliveryService to /workout. Update WebhookClient.post() calls in all services. Fix crash on invalid URL.

title: Fix silence timer cleanup on short utterance early return
description: In VoiceEngine.emitUtteranceIfValid(), when the transcript fails the minimumTranscriptLength check (< 3 chars), the function returns early WITHOUT invalidating the silence timer. This leaves the system in limbo — no utterance emitted, no timer reset. Fix: invalidate and reset the silence timer on early return so the next speech event properly re-arms detection.

title: Session picker not populated from gateway — fix RPC and filter to discord + main sessions
description:
The session picker only shows the static 'Main' fallback. The sessions.list RPC either fails silently or returns sessions that are all filtered out.

## Root Cause to Investigate
1. Is the RPC call actually being made? (add logging in loadSessions)
2. Is the gateway responding with sessions? (log the raw response)
3. Is operator.read scope being granted? (log hello-ok auth.scopes from gateway response)

## Filter Requirements
Show ONLY these session types:
- Sessions where key == 'agent:main:main' (always shown, labeled 'Main')
- Sessions where key contains ':discord:' (Discord channels, labeled by channel name or derivedTitle)
- Exclude: cron sessions, hook sessions, sub-agent sessions, telegram, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add logging in loadSessions() that prints: number of sessions returned, and each session key
- [ ] Log the hello-ok payload to confirm operator.read is in auth.scopes
- [ ] Filter: sessions = result.filter { /bin/zsh.key == 'agent:main:main' || /bin/zsh.key.contains(':discord:') }
- [ ] Static 'Main' fallback always present (even if RPC fails)
- [ ] After fix: picker shows 'Main' + Discord channel sessions from the gateway
- [ ] Verify by running app and checking logs: sessions.list returns >0 results

## Files
Zaap/Zaap/SessionPickerViewModel.swift
Zaap/Zaap/GatewayConnection.swift

title: Connect gateway eagerly on voice tab appear to populate session picker
description: Session picker is empty because the gateway only connects when the user taps the mic (startSession). Sessions load in gatewayDidConnect, but nothing triggers a connection until mic tap. Fix: when VoiceChatView appears and device is paired, connect the gateway immediately so gatewayDidConnect fires and loadSessions() populates the picker before the user interacts. The gateway URL is available from SettingsManager.shared.voiceWebSocketURL.

title: Pre-push hook simulator device name should be configurable

title: Funnel persistence — tailscale serve overwrites funnel on gateway restart

Need a post-restart hook or launchd script that runs 'tailscale funnel --bg --set-path / http://127.0.0.1:18789' after gateway starts. This is a zane-setup issue, not a Zaap code issue — but it blocks Zaap from working reliably.

title: Debug TTS panel with The Raven, word highlighting, and audio level meter
description: Second debug panel (under gear menu): TTS diagnostics. Displays first three verses of Poe's The Raven with a play/pause button. When playing, the currently spoken word is highlighted in real-time (via AVSpeechSynthesizerDelegate willSpeakRangeOfSpeechString). A vertical audio level meter on the side shows speaker output level — critical for assessing echo bleed when used alongside the STT debug panel. Both panels can be active simultaneously to visualize the full echo loop: TTS output vs what STT picks up.

notes: Added Bearer token to WebSocket HTTP upgrade request. Branch: fix/bearer-token-websocket.

title: Hide 'Recognition request was canceled' error from chat transcript
description:
When the speech recognizer is canceled (e.g. during stopListening()), the error 'Recognition request was canceled' appears as a yellow warning in the chat transcript. This is an internal implementation detail and must not be shown to the user.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Grep VoiceEngine.swift and VoiceChatViewModel.swift — 'Recognition request was canceled' (or recognitionFailed errors from cancellations) must be swallowed, not forwarded to the UI
- [ ] In VoiceEngine, filter out errors where error.localizedDescription contains 'canceled' or error code 301 (kAFAssistantErrorDomain canceled)
- [ ] No yellow warning bubbles in the chat from speech recognizer cancellations

title: Echo cancellation not working — mic still picks up speaker output
description:
Despite setting AVAudioSession mode to .voiceChat, TTS speaker output is still fed back into the microphone.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Run the app, start a voice session, speak a prompt, let the assistant respond via TTS — the assistant must NOT hear its own voice and respond again
- [ ] Verify in VoiceEngineAdapters.swift that setCategory is called with mode: .voiceChat (grep for voiceChat)
- [ ] Verify the audio session is configured BEFORE audioEngine.start() is called
- [ ] If voiceChat mode alone is insufficient: implement mic muting while AVSpeechSynthesizer is speaking — subscribe to AVSpeechSynthesizerDelegate willSpeakRangeOfSpeechString and didFinish; call audioEngine.pause()/resume() or inputNode volume = 0/1
- [ ] Add a test or log line confirming AEC is active

## Definition of Done
Worker must paste the relevant code diff AND confirm echo no longer occurs by describing what changed and why it prevents feedback.

Fix all force unwraps, ensure main-thread initialization, add failure-path tests.

title: Remove navigation titles from all tabs (Voice, Settings, Dashboard)

title: Show pending retry count at top of request log

- [ ] Count updates live as queue drains

## Dependency
Blocked by zaap-qpt

title: Fix mic restarting on gateway reconnect when conversation mode is off
description:
When the user toggles conversation mode off (mic button shows blue/unfilled), the voice engine stops correctly. However, if the gateway reconnects (network blip, WebSocket reconnection), gatewayDidConnect() at VoiceChatCoordinator.swift:313-324 restarts the mic unconditionally as long as isSessionActive is true — it does not check isConversationModeOn.

Repro:
1. Start a voice session (mic on, conversation mode active)
2. Toggle conversation mode off (tap mic button — goes blue)
3. Wait for a gateway reconnect (or trigger a network blip)
4. Mic restarts and picks up ambient audio (e.g. YouTube playing)

Root cause — VoiceChatCoordinator.swift:319:
  guard isSessionActive else { return }
Only checks isSessionActive, not isConversationModeOn.

Fix: Change line 319 to:
  guard isSessionActive, isConversationModeOn else { return }

This ensures the mic only restarts on reconnect if the user actually has conversation mode on.

title: Sleep webhook — POST morning sleep summary to /hooks/health
CRASH FIX: Replace background URLSession async/await with standard URLSession
description:
Root cause identified from crash report (Zaap-2026-02-19-102926.ips):

CRASH: NSURLBackgroundSession throws ObjC exception when using async/await upload(for:fromFile:). This is an iOS limitation — background sessions do NOT support Swift concurrency async/await on real devices (works in simulator).

Stack trace: __NSURLBackgroundSession _onqueue_dummyTaskForClass → objc_exception_throw → SIGABRT

FIX: Replace the background URLSession in WebhookClient with a standard URLSession.default. Use upload(for:from:) with Data instead of fromFile. Background delivery can be revisited later with proper delegate-based approach if needed.

Files to change:
- WebhookClient.swift: Remove backgroundSession, use foregroundSession for everything
- Remove SessionDelegate class (no longer needed without background session)

States: Idle (mic icon, tap to start), Listening (animated waveform, partial transcript shown), Processing (thinking indicator), Speaking (animated speaker, response text shown). Shows conversation log (user utterances + agent responses). Requires GatewayConnection + VoiceEngine + ResponseSpeaker.

The scale should auto-size based on the max count in the data. Keep the chart clean — gridlines subtle (opacity ~0.3), labels in secondary color.

title: Add SpO2 (Blood Oxygen) tracking: ObservedHealthDataType, delivery service, and /hooks/spo2 gateway route
Settings: Group gateway bearer token with hostname and hooks token, add dev/prod toggle
HealthKit observer queries — background delivery triggered by data changes
description:
Replace one-shot-on-launch delivery with HKObserverQuery + enableBackgroundDelivery for all HealthKit types. When the Apple Watch or any app writes new data, iOS wakes Zaap and it fetches+delivers immediately.

Implementation:
- Heart rate: .immediate — fires as watch records samples
- Workouts: .immediate — fires when workout session ends
- Activity/Steps: .hourly — steps update constantly, hourly batching is fine
- Sleep: .immediate — fires when sleep session is written

Add HealthKitObserverService that:
1. Calls enableBackgroundDelivery(for:frequency:) for each type
2. Registers HKObserverQuery for each type
3. On query callback: fetch latest data and call the relevant delivery service
4. Calls the completion handler to tell iOS the background task is done (critical — or iOS throttles future wakes)

Info.plist: add 'fetch' to UIBackgroundModes if not already present.

This replaces the current deliverLatest() on-launch pattern for sleep/activity. Location already uses CLLocationManager significant changes (keep as-is).

title: No barge-in: user cannot interrupt TTS by speaking
description:
When the agent is speaking a long response via TTS, the user cannot interrupt by speaking. The mic is explicitly turned off during TTS playback to avoid echo pickup (VoiceChatCoordinator.swift:100-102). The mic only restarts after TTS finishes and a 0.5s delay.

This means for long responses, the user must sit through the entire TTS output before they can speak again. There is no barge-in mechanism at all.

Possible approaches:
- Keep the mic active during TTS and rely on VPIO echo cancellation (the comment says 'hardware AEC insufficient on device' — but this may have improved, or could be tested)
- Use audio level monitoring on the input to detect when the user is speaking louder than the TTS output, and interrupt TTS
- Add a tap-to-interrupt gesture (tap anywhere on screen during TTS to silence it and re-enable mic)
- Use the TTSAudioPlayer (AVSpeechSynthesizer.write() + AVAudioPlayerNode) which routes through the shared AVAudioEngine with VPIO, enabling proper echo cancellation so the mic can stay on

Note: TTSAudioPlayer already exists at Zaap/Zaap/TTSAudioPlayer.swift but is NOT wired into the ResponseSpeaker or coordinator. It was built for diagnostics. Switching ResponseSpeaker to use it could enable keeping the mic on during TTS.

Key files:
- VoiceChatCoordinator.swift (speaker.onStateChange callback, lines 93-107)
- ResponseSpeaker.swift (uses AVSpeechSynthesizer.speak() directly)
- TTSAudioPlayer.swift (alternative approach using write() + AVAudioPlayerNode)
- VoiceEngineAdapters.swift (RealAudioEngineProvider with VPIO)

title: Replace voice connection handshake with new WebSocket protocol
description:
Implement the new WebSocket handshake protocol to replace the current voice connection handshake.

## Current State
The existing voice connection handshake needs to be replaced with a more robust WebSocket protocol.

## New Protocol Requirements

### Step 1: WebSocket Connection
- Connect to: wss://zanebot.tail66e5f8.ts.net

### Step 2: Challenge Handling  
- Receive challenge with nonce and timestamp from gateway
- Save nonce for signature generation

### Step 3: Device Identity (Ed25519)
- Generate Ed25519 keypair (one-time, persistent)
- deviceId = SHA-256 of raw public key bytes (hex)
- publicKey = raw 32-byte public key, base64url-encoded

### Step 4: Signature Generation (v3)
- Build payload: v3|deviceId|clientId|clientMode|role|scopes|signedAtMs|token|nonce|platform|deviceFamily
- Sign with Ed25519 private key
- Base64url encode signature

### Step 5: Connect Request
- Send structured connect request with auth, device, and signature details
- Handle pairing flow for new devices (code 1008)
- Store deviceToken for future connections

### Key Implementation Notes
- Use CryptoKit Curve25519.Signing for Ed25519 in Swift
- Store keypair in Keychain permanently  
- publicKey is raw 32 bytes base64url (not PEM)
- All string fields lowercase ASCII
- deviceToken can replace bearer token on subsequent connects

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WebSocket connection established
- [ ] Ed25519 keypair generation and storage
- [ ] Challenge/response flow implemented
- [ ] Signature generation working
- [ ] Connect request properly formatted
- [ ] Pairing flow handled
- [ ] deviceToken persistence
- [ ] Error handling for connection failures
notes: Worker made partial changes (v2→v3 signature) but missed the main requirement: implementing new WebSocket handshake to replace current voice connection. Need full protocol replacement per original spec.

title: Persistent webhook retry queue — retry failed pushes on reconnect
description:
Failed webhook deliveries are currently logged and forgotten. They need to be queued and retried.

## Requirements
1. **Persistent queue**: Failed POSTs are stored to disk (SwiftData or a simple JSON/JSONL file) with enough info to replay them — URL path, payload data, timestamp of original attempt
2. **Survives app lifecycle**: Queue persists through app close, reopen, update, reinstall (use app's Documents directory or SwiftData store)
3. **Drain on success**: When any webhook POST succeeds, begin draining the queue at a throttled rate — 1 item every 5 seconds, in order of original attempt time
4. **Deduplication**: Don't re-queue items that are already queued (match on payload hash or timestamp+type)
5. **Max queue size**: Cap at 500 entries — drop oldest if exceeded to prevent unbounded growth
6. **Per-item TTL**: Drop items older than 7 days — stale health data is not useful

## Architecture
- New class:  (wraps SwiftData or file-based persistence)
-  consults the queue: after any successful POST, triggers drain
-  on failure: enqueues the failed item
- Drain runs as a background Task, fires 1 item per 5s, stops when queue is empty or a drain item fails

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WebhookRetryQueue persists items to disk (SwiftData model or JSONL in Documents/)
- [ ] Failed POST → item added to queue with: path, payload, timestamp
- [ ] Successful POST → drain begins (if queue non-empty), 1 item per 5s
- [ ] Drain stops if an item fails (network still down), resumes on next success
- [ ] Items older than 7 days are pruned on app launch
- [ ] Queue capped at 500 items
- [ ] Unit tests for: enqueue, drain throttle, TTL pruning, max size cap
- [ ] Existing DeliveryLogService shows 'queued for retry' status for retried items

title: Voice should automatically use selected TTS voice from settings instead of separate selection
'No speech detected' warning still appears intermittently
description:
The zaap-yxj fix suppresses cold-start recognition errors (before any partial has been received). However, 'No speech detected' can still appear after the cold-start period — once hasReceivedPartial is true, subsequent recognition errors are no longer suppressed.

Scenario: User speaks, partials are received (hasReceivedPartial = true). Recognition restarts (e.g. after isFinal). The new recognition task fires a 'No speech detected' error. Since hasReceivedPartial is still true from the previous task, the error is NOT suppressed and reaches the UI via onError -> recognitionFailed -> viewModel.updatePartialTranscript.

The cold-start suppression at VoiceEngine.swift:174-178 only guards on hasReceivedPartial, which is set to true permanently after the first partial and never reset:
  guard self.hasReceivedPartial else {
      self.logHandler("suppressing cold-start recognition error")
      return
  }

Fix: Reset hasReceivedPartial to false in restartRecognition() so that each new recognition task gets its own grace period. Or suppress 'No speech detected' errors universally (they are informational, not actionable by the user).

Key files:
- VoiceEngine.swift (hasReceivedPartial, lines 100/186-190/174-178, restartRecognition)

title: Mic button off should stop both listening and speaking
description:
When the user taps the mic button to turn it off, it should stop both:
1. Listening (voice input / speech recognition)
2. Speaking (TTS / response playback)

Currently stopSession() stops listening and calls speaker.interrupt() but the speaking may continue. The mic button should be a hard stop for the entire voice interaction — nothing in, nothing out.

title: Session switch while mic active: conversation log doesn't reload
description:
When switching sessions while the mic is active (voice chat running), the conversation log does not update to show the new session's message history.

## Expected Behavior
- When selectedSessionKey changes, the conversation log clears and reloads for the new session
- If mic is active when session switches, mic should stop, session context should reset, then mic restarts (or stays stopped — TBD)

## Actual Behavior
- Conversation log shows stale messages from the previous session
- New session's history is not loaded

## Likely Cause
- VoiceChatCoordinator or VoiceChatViewModel not observing selectedSessionKey changes
- No clear/reload triggered when session key changes mid-session

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Switching sessions clears the conversation log
- [ ] New session's recent history is loaded/displayed after switch
- [ ] If mic was active, it stops cleanly on session switch (no orphaned audio)
- [ ] No crash or stuck mic state
- [ ] Unit test covering session switch while active

description:

2. Filter to Discord sessions only (channel type = discord)

- Zaap/Zaap/SessionPickerView.swift

title: Fix 'No speech detected' on first mic tap (cold-start)
description:
When the user first taps the mic button, Apple's SFSpeechRecognizer often fires a recognition error before audio is fully flowing through the VPIO audio engine. This surfaces as a warning symbol with 'No speech detected' in the partial transcript area (via VoiceChatCoordinator's onError -> recognitionFailed path). The user has to tap the mic twice to get it working.

Possible fixes:
- Add a warm-up grace period after startListening() during which recognition errors are suppressed
- Pre-warm the audio session before the first recognition task starts
- Extend the watchdog timer on the initial recognition attempt
- Suppress the 'no speech' error specifically during the first N seconds after mic activation

Key files:
- VoiceEngine.swift (watchdog timer, error handling, startListening)
- VoiceChatCoordinator.swift (onError callback, lines 117-128)
- VoiceEngineAdapters.swift (RealAudioSessionConfigurator, VPIO setup)
close_reason: Superseded by zaap-brq (cold-start) and zaap-w8d (intermittent no-speech). The original cold-start suppression fix landed but didn't fully solve the problem.

title: Design: Voice chat — research gateway WS protocol and architect voice conversation feature
description:
Research and design the voice conversation feature for Zaap.

Goals:
- User opens voice tab, starts talking (no push-to-talk — use voice activity detection)
- SFSpeechRecognizer transcribes speech, detects end-of-utterance on silence
- Transcript sent to OpenClaw gateway, response streamed back
- AVSpeechSynthesizer speaks the response
- Conversation stays open for back-and-forth

Research needed:
1. Gateway WebSocket protocol — can Zaap connect as a node? The Android app already does this (ws://<host>:18789 with pairing). If Zaap implements the node protocol, it gets bidirectional chat for free.
2. Alternative: custom WS endpoint or synchronous hook response
3. iOS constraints: SFSpeechRecognizer continuous mode, background audio session, VAD silence detection thresholds
4. TTS voice selection — AVSpeechSynthesizer voices vs ElevenLabs API

Deliverable: Architecture doc in .braids/iterations/ with sequence diagrams, protocol choice, and implementation plan broken into sub-beads.

title: Add operator WebSocket connection for session listing and chat
description:
The app connects to the gateway as role 'node', but sessions.list requires role 'operator' with scope 'operator.read'. The official openclaw iOS app handles this with two separate WebSocket connections — one as node (for device capabilities like node.event), one as operator (for sessions.list, chat.send, etc.).

## Root Cause
GatewayConnection connects with role:'node' and scopes:['operator.read']. When the coordinator calls sessions.list after hello-ok, the gateway rejects it with INVALID_REQUEST 'unauthorized role: node'. This error then cascades into the pairing bug (see zaap-bgg).

## Required Changes
1. Add a second GatewayConnection (or refactor to support dual connections) — one for node role, one for operator role
2. The operator connection needs role:'operator', scopes:['operator.read'] (minimum), and its own device token (per-role tokens in openclaw)
3. Route sessions.list (and future chat.send, chat.history) through the operator connection
4. Route node.event (voice transcripts) through the node connection
5. The first operator connect will trigger a one-time 'role-upgrade' re-pairing on the gateway — handle this gracefully
6. Store operator device token separately from node device token (tokens are per-role in openclaw)

## Reference
- openclaw role policy: src/gateway/role-policy.ts — only 'operator' and 'node' roles exist
- openclaw method scopes: src/gateway/method-scopes.ts — sessions.list requires operator role + operator.read scope
- openclaw iOS app: apps/ios/Sources/Model/NodeAppModel.swift — uses two connections (nodeGateway + operatorGateway)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] sessions.list is sent over an operator-role connection and succeeds
- [ ] voice transcripts (node.event) are sent over the node-role connection and succeed
- [ ] No 'unauthorized role: node' errors in logs after connecting
- [ ] Role-upgrade pairing flow works on first connect (one-time approval)
- [ ] Both device tokens stored and retrieved correctly
- [ ] All existing tests pass + new tests for dual-connection behavior

title: Conversation mode toggle — persistent mic-hot voice chat
description: Replace the current one-shot push-to-talk with a persistent conversation mode toggle. Tap mic once to enter conversation mode (red), tap again to exit (blue). When on: mic stays hot (no muting during TTS), TTS speaks responses, auto-cycles back to listening after TTS finishes. When off: no STT, no TTS. Trust iOS .voiceChat hardware AEC instead of muting mic. Sim has no AEC — test on real device.
